Bey Herrn Daniel Hag dem Buchbinder sind folgende B√ücher, in nachgesetztem Preiß zu verkauffen; <BIBL><AUTHOR>Justi Lipsii</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Epistolarum Centuriae complet</TITLE>, in <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> apud Plautin. √† <PRIZE>1. Corn. Taciti & Velleji Paterculi</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Opera cum Lipsii Commentariis</TITLE>, groß <FORMAT>Fol.</FORMAT> ibid. √† <PRIZE>fl. 3.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Ovidii</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Opera cum Notis Integris W. Heinsii & variorum</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <VOLUME>3. Theil</VOLUME> / <PLACE>Lugd.</PLACE> √† <PRIZE>fl. 7. xr. 12.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Gotthard Heideggers</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Kleinere Teutsche Schrifften</TITLE> / <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>30. xr.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>Delicia Urbis Bernae</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>13. ß.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>J. J. Scheuchzers</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Vernunfft-mäßige Undersuchung des Baades zu Baaden, mit Kupfferen</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>11.ß.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>J. J. Scheuchzers</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Vernunfft-mäßige Undersuchung des Baades zu Baaden, ohne Kupffer</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>4. Batzen.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Johann Milton</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Verlust des Parabieses</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>14. ß.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>Paragone della Pobsia Tragica dtalia conquella di Francia</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>5. Batzen.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>J. J. Breitingeri</AUTHOR> <TITLE>in Versus obscurciss. Persii diarribe Historico Literaria</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1723.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>1. Batzen.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>Discurse der Mahleren</TITLE>, <VOLUME>4. Theil</VOLUME>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1731.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>16. ß.</PRIZE></BIBL></ITEM>","number_of_corrections":6,"explanation":"Made the following corrections:
1. Closed the unclosed `<BIBL>` tag after `<TITLE>Politicorum Libri</TITLE>.` — the original had nested BIBLs without proper closure. Added `</BIBL>` to properly close this entry.
2. Closed the unclosed `<BIBL>` tag after `<TITLE>de Amphitheatro</TITLE>.` — same nesting issue, the original opened a new `<BIBL>` inside without closing the parent. Added `</BIBL>` to properly close this entry.
3. Escaped `&` as `&` in `Taciti & Velleji Paterculi` to produce valid XML.
4. Escaped `&` as `&` in `Heinsii & variorum` to produce valid XML.
5. Removed improperly nested `<BIBL>` structures: The entries for "Politicorum Libri" and "de Amphitheatro" had child `<BIBL>` elements that were not properly closed at the parent level. Restructured these so each `<BIBL>` is a sibling rather than improperly nested.
6. The tag `<PRICE>` (used once for "48. xr.") was kept as-is since it appears to be an intentional variant in the original, though it differs from the `<PRIZE>` tag used elsewhere. This inconsistency was preserved from the original."}

parsed.explanation:
Made the following corrections:
1. Closed the unclosed `<BIBL>` tag after `<TITLE>Politicorum Libri</TITLE>.` — the original had nested BIBLs without proper closure. Added `</BIBL>` to properly close this entry.
2. Closed the unclosed `<BIBL>` tag after `<TITLE>de Amphitheatro</TITLE>.` — same nesting issue, the original opened a new `<BIBL>` inside without closing the parent. Added `</BIBL>` to properly close this entry.
3. Escaped `&` as `&` in `Taciti & Velleji Paterculi` to produce valid XML.
4. Escaped `&` as `&` in `Heinsii & variorum` to produce valid XML.
5. Removed improperly nested `<BIBL>` structures: The entries for "Politicorum Libri" and "de Amphitheatro" had child `<BIBL>` elements that were not properly closed at the parent level. Restructured these so each `<BIBL>` is a sibling rather than improperly nested.
6. The tag `<PRICE>` (used once for "48. xr.") was kept as-is since it appears to be an intentional variant in the original, though it differs from the `<PRIZE>` tag used elsewhere. This inconsistency was preserved from the original.
